chapter_goal: Recover Gaussian curvature from first fundamental form data and inspect why local isometries preserve it.
computational_checks: exact metric curvature identities for five metrics
explicit first/second form checks for plane, cylinder, sphere, catenoid, and helicoid
fixed-metric equality checks for plane/cylinder and catenoid/helicoid
artifact existence, size, and nonblank image checks
concept_inventory: first and second fundamental forms as compatible data
Christoffel symbols as metric-derived connection coefficients
local isometry and bending without stretching
Chebyshev nets and sine-Gordon compatibility for K=-1
geodesic local diffeomorphisms and central projection

library_routing_table.why: the theorem is an identity among derivatives of E,F,G and Christoffel symbols

library_routing_table.representation: rotatable 3D surface comparison plus static thumbnail
library_routing_table.why: surface shape and grid bending are easier to inspect in 3D
the chapter proof is a dependency chain from metric data to K
the ODE/PDE reductions are inspectable as functions and residuals

library_routing_table.representation: 3D central projection diagram and speed check
library_routing_table.why: the map sends great-circle arcs to straight lines while changing parameter speed

orientation_notes: 10.1 relates first and second fundamental forms through Codazzi-Mainardi and Gauss equations.
10.2 deduces Theorema Egregium and gives metric-only curvature formulas and isometry obstructions.
10.3 reduces constant curvature patches to plane, sphere, pseudosphere models and the sine-Gordon equation for K=-1.
10.4 studies geodesic maps, including central projection and Beltrami's constant-curvature criterion.
source_span_read.textbook: Andrew Pressley, Elementary Differential Geometry, Second Edition

visual_sequence.inspection_target: compare sqrt(G), Gamma^2_12, and K for the sphere, pseudosphere metric, and helicoid radial metric
visual_sequence.validation: symbolic residual K_metric - K_expected is zero

visual_sequence.inspection_target: follow which assumptions feed Codazzi, Gauss, and the intrinsic K conclusion
visual_sequence.validation: graph has directed path metric -> Christoffel -> K

visual_sequence.inspection_target: same parameter grids bend into different embeddings
visual_sequence.validation: symbolic first-form matrices match within each pair

visual_sequence.inspection_target: rotate catenoid and helicoid sharing cosh(u)^2(du^2+dv^2)
see g(u) for K=0,1,-1 and a sine-Gordon solution in the K=-1 Chebyshev form
great-circle arc projects to a straight line with nonconstant speed
